parsed.summary: Cannot complete a defensible adversarial review — only the diff stat and commit log were provided, not the actual diff content. Two structural risks are visible: (1) scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely deleted, creating a dangling-import crash risk if anything still references it, and (2) 6600+ lines across 84 files introduce new retry, error-dispatch, and job-tracking subsystems whose integration correctness cannot be verified from metadata alone. Ship approval requires reading the actual code.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs risks unresolved ESM imports at runtime
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely removed (67 lines deleted per diff stat). If any remaining module still contains `import … from './process.mjs'` or `'../lib/process.mjs'`, Node will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at load time with no fallback. ESM static imports fail before any user code runs, producing an opaque crash. scripts/check-imports.mjs was modified (±5 lines) which may address this, but without confirming the import graph, this is an unvalidated kill-switch risk.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -rn "process\.mjs" scripts/` to confirm zero remaining references. If check-imports.mjs validates this, add a comment documenting the validation. Add the check to CI (pr-check.yml) so regressions are caught automatically.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) lack integration-level verification against modified glm-client.mjs
findings.body: The diff adds two new critical-path modules (retry with backoff, error dispatch for BigModel) and heavily modifies glm-client.mjs (±155 lines). Unit tests exist for each in isolation (retry.test.mjs 352 lines, bigmodel-errors.test.mjs 189 lines), but the interaction surface — retry logic calling back into glm-client, error dispatch deciding retry-vs-fail, tracked-jobs.mjs updating state on exhausted retries — is not verifiable from the summary. A bug in this chain could cause silent job loss (retry exhausts without persisting failure state) or indefinite hangs (backoff never terminates).
findings.recommendation: Before ship: (1) verify retry.mjs has a hard max-attempts ceiling that cannot be exceeded, (2) confirm glm-client.mjs propagates retry exhaustion to tracked-jobs.mjs as a terminal failure state, (3) add at least one integration test covering the full retry→exhaust→state-transition path.

findings.title: Scope and version-spread make rollback safety uncertain
findings.body: The changeset spans v0.4.0→v0.4.6 across 84 files with 6600+ additions — effectively 6 minor releases bundled into one diff. If a defect is discovered post-ship, rollback to v0.4.0 reverts all 6 releases worth of fixes. State files (state.mjs modified ±32 lines), job tracking (tracked-jobs.mjs ±78 lines), and preset config (preset-config.mjs ±15 lines) may have schema or format changes that make rolling back unsafe (v0.4.6 persisted state unreadable by v0.4.0).
findings.recommendation: Verify that state.mjs persisted data formats are backward-compatible with v0.4.0 readers, or that a migration path exists. Document rollback procedure and any state-format incompatibilities in release_card.md.

next_steps: Provide the actual diff content (git diff v0.4.0..HEAD) so line-level adversarial review can be completed
Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/` to confirm the deleted module has zero remaining importers
Inspect retry.mjs for hard max-attempts ceiling and confirm exhausted retries produce a terminal persisted state
Verify state.mjs data format backward compatibility or document migration/rollback constraints
Confirm tracked-jobs.mjs cleans up resources when retry exhausts (no leaked temp files, zombie processes, or orphaned state)
